title: MCP server: Add runtime token refresh mechanism
body:
## Problem

The MCP server loads auth tokens once at startup. If:
- User logs in after the MCP server starts
- Token expires while server is running
- Token is refreshed by CLI but server has stale copy

The server will not pick up these changes and may return auth errors until restarted.

## Related PR

Raised in code review for #268

## Proposed Solution

Implement on-demand auth loading in `RequireAuth()`:
1. Check if `s.auth` is nil or expired
2. Reload from `TokenStore` to pick up CLI-refreshed tokens
3. If still expired and have refresh token, attempt refresh
4. Cache result with short TTL to avoid file reads on every request

This allows auth changes to take effect without restarting the MCP server.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] MCP server picks up tokens saved by `bop auth login` without restart
- [ ] Token refresh works during long-running MCP sessions
- [ ] No performance regression from excessive file reads
